entries.content: The user requested to create a web-based Sokoban game in the /Users/bootscoder/Desktop/project-code-agent/lumen/examples directory.
The assistant suggested structuring the web-based Sokoban game with three files: sokoban.html for structure, sokoban.css for styles, and sokoban.js for logic.
The suggested file structure for the Sokoban game is to place sokoban.html, sokoban.css, and sokoban.js under the examples/ directory.
The project uses three files: wuziqi.html, wuziqi.css, and wuziqi.js, located in the examples/ directory.
The user prefers to follow a gated review process with explicit design approval before implementation.
The design phase requires proposing function or class signatures with names, parameters, return types, and one-line responsibilities, but no implementation bodies.
The project follows a data flow where user clicks are handled by handleClick, which updates the board and winner, then triggers drawPieces, checkWin, updateStatus, and restartGame as needed.
The user prefers to implement one function at a time during the implementation phase.
After implementing each function, the user wants an explanation of the function's inputs, data transformation, and outputs.
After each function implementation and explanation, the user wants to call request_approval with the phase, function name, and a summary.
After explanation, the user calls request_approval with the phase, function name, and a summary.
User prefers to implement one function at a time and receive an explanation of its inputs, transformation, and outputs after each implementation.
User follows a convention of calling 'request_approval' after each function implementation with phase, title, and summary.
The project has seven approved core functions: drawBoard, drawPieces, initBoard, handleClick, checkWin, restartGame, updateStatus.

[CONSOLIDATED] The user is developing a web-based Sokoban game in the /Users/bootscoder/Desktop/project-code-agent/lumen/examples directory, following a structure modeled after an existing project that uses three files—HTML, CSS, and JS—for organization. The assistant recommended placing sokoban.html, sokoban.css, and sokoban.js in the examples/ directory, mirroring the setup of the wuziqi.* files already present. The user has established a gated review workflow, requiring explicit design approval before moving to implementation, and mandates that the design phase consist of proposing function or class signatures with names, parameters, return types, and concise responsibilities, but without implementation bodies. During implementation, the user prefers to work on one function at a time, receiving a detailed explanation of each function’s inputs, data transformation, and outputs upon completion, and then consistently calls request_approval with the phase, function name, and a summary. The project’s data flow pattern involves user clicks being processed by handleClick, which updates the board and winner, and then triggers drawPieces, checkWin, updateStatus, and restartGame as appropriate. Seven core functions—drawBoard, drawPieces, initBoard, handleClick, checkWin, restartGame, and updateStatus—have already received design approval, and the user is proceeding to implement them sequentially under this structured review process.
The project files for the Gomoku (五子棋) game are located at /Users/bootscoder/Desktop/project-code-agent/lumen/examples/ and include wuziqi.html, wuziqi.css, and wuziqi.js.
The user prefers to implement one function at a time and provide an explanation of inputs, transformation, and outputs before requesting approval.
